channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump's halt on military aid will hurt Ukraine's defenses. But it may not be fatal
item.description: The U.S. has been Ukraine’s biggest military backer since Russia’s full-scale invasion began three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indigenous migrants in northern Colombia battle worsening droughts and floods
item.description: In La Guajira, Colombia, the Wayuu people face worsening climate impacts, with droughts and intense rains causing food insecurity, flooding, and displacement.
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, The Hill

item.title: Department of Education clarifies school DEI guidelines, saying Black History Month doesn't violate order
item.description: The Department of Education clarified that celebrating Black History Month is not in violation of an order warning states to remove DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Manafort: Peace is not in Zelensky's interest
item.description: Manafort, who advised former Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych, said Zelenskyy has political motivations for prolonging the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, The Hill

item.title: Macron to discuss nuclear deterrence with European allies. A look at France's unique strategy
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron says he’s ready to start discussions on nuclear deterrence with European allies.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: A Pakistani official says residents have fled a border area with Afghanistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of the morning.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UAE Supreme Court upholds sentences issued in mass trial of dissidents widely criticized abroad
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates' Federal Supreme Court rejected appeals Tuesday on behalf of dozens of dissidents convicted in a mass trial that faced widespread international criticism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to resume using a nasal tube for oxygen, rather than a ventilation mask, as he continued to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigators will question a driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in Germany, killing 2
item.description: MANNHEIM, Germany (AP) — Investigators on Tuesday will question a driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, killing two people and injuring 11.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and MICHAEL PROBST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine fighter jet carrying 2 pilots goes missing during mission against insurgents in southern province
item.description: A Philippine air force fighter jet went missing during a night combat assault in support of ground forces who were battling insurgents in a southern province.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Timber over Tinder: German forest has connected lovers for over a century
item.description: Known as “Bräutigamseiche” in German, the Bridegroom’s Oak has a famous knothole that's been used as a mailbox since 1892.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The economy and AI are likely to be among the top issues for China’s legislature
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Harnessing the power of artificial intelligence and shoring up the economy are among the issues expected to top the agenda when China’s legislature meets Wednesday in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's pause on military aid alarms Ukrainians but Kyiv digs in on peace terms
item.description: Ukrainians are alarmed and apprehensive about a U.S. decision to pause military aid critical to their fight against Russia’s invasion, as a rift deepens between Kyiv and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Greenlanders resisting Trump's talk of acquiring their homeland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenlanders have been pushed into the global spotlight in the weeks since U.S. President Donald Trump said America could take over their Arctic homeland. Most say they don’t want to be American. Many are worried and overwhelmed by the comments and the attention.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US tariffs take effect and Mexico, Canada and China retaliate with their own tariffs on the US
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico have gone into effect, kicking off a trade war with America's neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Paul Wiseman And Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Colombian president aims to reduce drug violence and weaken rebels by paying farmers to switch crops
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro announced a plan Monday to reduce coca planting in a northeastern region rattled by rebel attacks by paying farmers while they switch to legal crops.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim's sister threatens a response to a US carrier's deployment in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened Tuesday to launch high-profile provocations in response to the arrival of a U.S. aircraft carrier in South Korea and other U.S. military activities, which she slammed as “confrontation hysteria of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tuberville: Zelensky 'not even in the game' on Ukraine war talks
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville (R-Ala.), a staunch supporter of President Trump’s, said Russia and the United States will ultimately determine the fate of the war in Ukraine, saying the war-torn democracy’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, is “not even in the game.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Iranian government official who was key to 2015 nuclear deal tenders resignation under pressure
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — A former Iranian foreign minister who was key to the country's 2015 nuclear deal with world powers reportedly tendered his resignation Monday from the government of reformist President Masoud Pezeshkian, caving in to pressure from hard-liners.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car ramming in Mannheim, Germany, leaves 2 dead, multiple injured: reports
item.description: A car ramming incident left two people dead and multiple others injured in the city of Mannheim, Germany on Monday, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's Starmer says Europe is at ‘crossroads in history’ as leaders agree to steps to Ukraine peace
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer rallied his European counterparts Sunday to shore up their borders and throw their full weight behind Ukraine as he announced outlines of a plan to end Russia’s war.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cartel-plagued Mexican city pins hopes on Trump's anti-drug trafficking pressure
item.description: CULIACAN, Mexico (AP) — Before dawn, an elementary school principal in the capital of Mexico's Sinaloa state checks various chats on his phone for word of shootouts or other incidents. If there’s danger, he sends a message to his students’ parents suspending classes.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan’s volunteer military shrinks amid growing Chinese aggression
item.description: Taiwan's military continues to struggle with recruiting troops as it looks to new methods to boost troop strength. One such idea is to enlist migrants into the island's armed forces.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy says ire with Trump began with pro-Putin rhetoric
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy says comments made by Trump criticizing Ukraine have prompted his citizens to question what happened to the "friendship" that existed just weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rose Girone, oldest living Holocaust survivor, dies at 113
item.description: Rose Girone, the oldest living survivor of the Holocaust and advocate for sharing the stories of survivors, died on Monday in New York at age 113.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Denver developer shot dead at luxury Belize home in likely 'hit': Police
item.description: Boris Mannsfeld was found shot to death at The Villas at Cocoplum, a luxury residential community on the Caribbean country's southeastern coast, Belizean media outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation
